title: Chapter Eighty-Seven: Kneel
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

He had to become someone else.

*Not an Apostle. My martial might doesn't reach that level. Someone lesser. Someone cruel enough to be believed.*

He thought of Gao Zong-xue -- the Third Apostle's son, who had swaggered through Blood Flame Sect's headquarters checking Yeon-shin at every turn. The one who had died forcing energy absorption on a boy who hadn't even learned martial arts.

Yeon-shin remembered slicing his neck.

*Become him.*

He walked forward. Gait leisurely. Spine straight. He told himself that outside the main hall there was none more noble than himself. All others were worthless bugs. The self-suggestion settled over him like a second skin.

*It fits better than expected.*

He was puzzled by how comfortable it felt.

He approached Namgung Mi's seat and picked up a peach from the table. Lifted it to the demon mask's mouth without biting. A gesture of contempt -- treating her feast as his.

"I like the fruit's colour," he said through the rupturing harmonics. "This fruit is wasted on these lowly things here."

No one raised their heads. Not the escort agency owners. Not the chancellor. Not the martial artists who had been shouting their righteous indignation moments ago. They cowered before the bearing of a boy in a stolen robe.

*They kept silent before Virtuous Teacher Sect too. The same people. The same silence.*

Only the Namgung family warriors remained standing.

"A blood demon acts like a noble before me?" Namgung Mi crossed her legs, smiling. "Perhaps feeling inferior to the main fortress? Not knowing your place."

"Your tongue is long."

"What?"

"A head that will soon fall moves its mouth too much."

He threw the peach at her nape.

She stood without flinching. The fruit rolled to her feet. She crushed it under her shoe, slowly, deliberately.

"Kill him," she said.

White mist erupted from her body. The cold hit him like a wall -- not temperature but internal breaking force, a yin energy that drove into his meridians and began freezing them shut. His movements slowed immediately. True qi circulation dropped. Muscle response dulled.

*As warned.*

The Flourishing Spirit Flower warriors rushed in from every direction. Five of them. Sword Formation -- multiple swordsmen combining their techniques into a connected assault.

CLANG!

The first blade met his iron sword before he had fully drawn it. The vibration ran up his arm. He was slower now, the cold seeping deeper with every heartbeat.

"Your sword force is pathetic," he said calmly from within the mask.

He struck the man's jaw with the back of his left hand. The sound was loud and final. The warrior flew backward, eyes rolling white.

*I can manage without unique techniques.*

He advanced into the remaining four. They came at him from all directions -- left, right, behind, overhead. His meridians ached. His skin felt frost-bitten. But his sense of timing lived in a place the cold could not reach.

Once each. That was all it took.

He measured distance by instinct, evading five sword movements by the width of a finger, inserting his blade into each gap. One strike per warrior. Five openings, five bodies hitting the ground.

Blood ran down the iron sword. In the silence that followed, the pure blood robe seemed wrapped in killing intent.

Namgung Mi had not moved.

"Your eye technique training doesn't seem deep," she said, approaching. Her tone was relaxed. She had watched her warriors fall and calculated that the cold was doing its work. "Your sense of distance is excellent. You measure timing well. Innate?"

She walked toward him as if approaching a lover. Reached out one snow-white hand. Touched the demon mask.

"Let's see your ugly face first."

*Good.*

He had been using Three Samadhi True Fire since she took her first step -- vibrating his true qi at its smallest units, generating heat through motion rather than innate fire. The cold in his meridians crackled and burned away. His body thawed without her knowing.

He raised the Radiant Demon Technique. The upper dantian in his mind flashed. An invisible trajectory appeared -- a light net visible only to his eyes. The path started from his right elbow, barely moving his shoulder at the end.

SWISH.

Just before her fingers lifted the mask, he grabbed her neck.

It was a very natural movement. A scene of instantaneous superiority built from her overconfidence, Radiant Demon Technique's blind-spot approach, timing collapsed to nothing, and the sudden acceleration of strikes freed from frozen meridians.

Her smooth nape settled into his grip. Namgung Mi's eyes went wider than they had ever gone in her life.

"The noble of the world's blood lineage commands," he said, and sealed her blood points in the same motion -- the technique he had learned by suffering it himself at the Seventh Apostle's hands. The same true qi operation. The same result.

Paralysis.

"You lowly Namgung seed."

Something stirred in the Demon Roaring Blood Technique's flow. The energy drawn into its path amplified slightly -- as if the art itself rewarded contempt, as if domination was how blood technique was meant to be operated.

*This too is enlightenment.*

He filled his next words with every drop of contempt he could muster.

"Kneel."

She could not. Her body was his now.

"What are you doing, trash?"

"This... this..."

The boy in the demon mask laughed slowly, holding the Flourishing Spirit Flower Master by the throat. The pure blood robe's hem fluttered at his feet like scattered blood.

---
